---
title: "Regression Discontinuity"
---

# Scope

This note will explain regression discontinuity design as a local comparison around a known treatment cutoff [@cunningham2018regression; @gertler2010ch6regression].

# Planned Sections

## What The Method Is

- Running variable
- Threshold-based assignment
- Local average treatment effect at the cutoff

## When To Use It

- Treatment assignment changes sharply at a rule-based threshold
- Units near the cutoff are plausibly comparable
- The running variable is observed precisely

## Core Assumptions

- Continuity of potential outcomes at the cutoff
- No precise manipulation of the running variable
- Correct handling of bandwidth and functional form

## Data Requirements

- Running variable
- Known cutoff
- Sufficient density near the threshold
- Outcome measured after assignment

## Workflow

1. Verify the assignment rule and cutoff.
2. Plot outcomes against the running variable.
3. Choose a bandwidth.
4. Estimate local treatment effects.
5. Check sensitivity to bandwidth and polynomial choices.

## Diagnostics And Risks

- Density tests near the cutoff
- Covariate continuity checks
- Bandwidth sensitivity
- Local interpretation only

## Worked Example

Eligibility for a grant based on a score threshold, comparing units just above and below the cutoff.

## Software

- `rdrobust`
- `rddensity`
- `ggplot2`
